wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the GeneView project overview section focused on introducing this Python genomics visualization library. Explain its purpose as a specialized tool for creating attractive and informative genomics graphics built on matplotlib and the PyData stack. Document the project's architecture, key features including GWAS Manhattan plots, Q-Q plots, population genetics admixture visualization, and Venn diagrams. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners new to genomics visualization and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the genomics and bioinformatics domains. Provide practical examples demonstrating the library's core capabilities and explain how it integrates with the scientific Python ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the GeneView introduction section focused on explaining what GeneView is and its role in genomics data visualization. Explain that GeneView is a Python library specifically designed for creating attractive and informative genomics graphics built on top of matplotlib and the PyData stack. Document the library's purpose as a specialized tool for researchers and bioinformaticians to visualize complex genomics datasets. Include the project's philosophy of providing high-level abstractions for structuring grids of plots while maintaining flexibility for custom visualizations. Use terminology consistent with genomics and bioinformatics domains. Provide context about why traditional plotting approaches fall short for genomics data and how GeneView addresses these challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Manhattan Plot Visualization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for GeneView's Manhattan plot functionality focused on genome-wide association study visualization. Explain chromosome positioning algorithms, -log10 P-value scaling, significance threshold lines (suggestive line at 1e-5, genome-wide significance at 5e-8), and SNP annotation capabilities. Document parameter specifications including data column mapping (#CHROM, POS, P, ID), color customization, marker styling, and text annotation options. Cover advanced features like selective chromosome plotting (CHR parameter), LD block analysis for top SNP identification, and interactive text adjustment. Include practical examples showing basic plots, customized styling, threshold customization, and integration with GWAS workflow pipelines. Address common use cases, statistical interpretations, and performance considerations for large genomic datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Color Palette Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for GeneView's color palette management system. Explain the generate_colors_palette() function for matplotlib colormap integration, including parameter specifications for cmap selection, n_colors customization, and alpha transparency control. Document the integration with Circos color schemes for genomic visualization consistency, including predefined color palettes optimized for chromosome ideograms and population genetics plots. Cover XKCD RGB color mapping for accessible color choices and scientific visualization best practices. Include practical examples demonstrating palette selection strategies for different plot types, colorblind-friendly schemes, and custom color scheme creation. Address performance considerations for large-scale color generation and memory optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Genomic Data Formats
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for genomic data formats supported by GeneView. Explain VCF (Variant Call Format), PLINK binary formats, and .Q files used in population genetics analysis. Document the structure and content of each format, including header information, data columns, and quality metrics. Provide examples of how GeneView processes and interprets these formats. Include practical guidance on preparing datasets for visualization, handling missing data, and format conversions. Address common issues with data preprocessing and validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Jupyter Notebook Tutorials
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for GeneView Jupyter notebook tutorials focused on interactive learning and hands-on experimentation. Document each notebook's objectives, data requirements, and step-by-step execution workflow. Include detailed explanations of code cells, parameter variations, and customization options. Provide guidance on running notebooks locally, required dependencies, and expected outputs. Document common modifications and extensions to the basic examples. Include troubleshooting tips for notebook execution issues and environment setup problems. Address how to adapt each tutorial for different datasets and research scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for GeneView's core visualization functions including manhattanplot(), qqplot(), qqnorm(), admixtureplot(), and venn(). Document complete function signatures with parameter specifications, data type requirements, default values, and validation rules. Provide detailed usage examples for each function showing typical genomics data inputs, parameter combinations, and output customization options. Include parameter validation logic, error handling patterns, and integration with matplotlib backend. Document function relationships, optional dependencies, and performance considerations for large genomic datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: GWAS Analysis Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the GWAS Analysis Tools section focused on Genome-Wide Association Study visualization capabilities. Document the three main GWAS plotting functions: Manhattan plots for genome-wide SNP association visualization, Q-Q plots for P-value distribution analysis and statistical significance assessment, and LocusZoom-style regional association plots for fine-mapping analysis. For each tool, explain the biological significance, statistical methodology, parameter configuration options, and typical research applications. Include practical examples showing data preparation requirements, threshold settings, chromosome positioning strategies, and customization options. Document the relationship between different GWAS visualization types and their role in comprehensive genetic association study workflows. Address common use cases in GWAS analysis pipelines and integration with downstream functional genomics analyses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admixture Coefficient Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the admixture coefficient analysis functionality. Explain the biological concept of ancestry proportions, .Q file format requirements and structure, and how admixture coefficients represent genetic ancestry. Document the admixtureplot function parameters including data input formats, sample grouping options, and visualization customization. Detail the hierarchical clustering integration for optimal sample ordering, explaining different clustering methods and their impact on visualization interpretation. Include practical examples of data preparation workflows, population group assignment strategies, and statistical interpretation of admixture results. Address common data quality issues, missing data handling, and visualization optimization techniques for complex population structures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Venn Diagram Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Venn diagram functionality section. Thoroughly explain the implementation of multi-set intersection analysis supporting 2-6 sets, covering the automatic shape calculation algorithms and geometric optimization criteria for visual clarity. Document the mathematical foundations of Venn diagram construction, including circle positioning algorithms, overlap area calculations, and petal boundary determination. Explain the generate_petal_labels function for customizable labeling systems and the venn() function interface for creating publication-ready Venn diagrams. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing different set configurations, custom styling options, and integration patterns with genomics data analysis workflows. Address practical applications in comparative genomics, pathway analysis, and functional enrichment studies. Document parameter configuration options, output customization capabilities, and best practices for clear and informative Venn diagram presentations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Manhattan Plots
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Manhattan plot visualization focused on genome-wide SNP association analysis. Explain the biological significance of chromosome positioning, significance threshold lines (genome-wide and suggestive), and SNP annotation strategies. Document the implementation details including chromosome coordinate calculation, data preprocessing requirements, and custom threshold configuration. Cover parameter options for chromosome sorting, color schemes, point sizing, and label positioning. Include practical examples showing data preparation from GWAS summary statistics, threshold setting strategies, and interactive SNP highlighting. Address common research applications in GWAS analysis including locus identification, replication validation, and multi-trait comparison workflows. Document integration with genomic annotation databases and quality control filtering approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Color Palette Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Color Palette Management system focused on GeneView's color infrastructure. Document the built-in palette generation functions including Seaborn integration, Circos color scheme compatibility, and XKCD RGB mappings. Explain the color palette selection strategies, theme consistency mechanisms, and customization options for different visualization types. Cover the palette factory pattern implementation, automatic color assignment for multiple populations, and harmonious color schemes for genomic data visualization. Include practical examples of palette creation, custom color scheme development, and integration with matplotlib styling. Document the color accessibility considerations, contrast optimization for different visualizations, and export functionality for sharing color schemes. Address performance optimization for large-scale color operations and extensibility for adding new palette types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Installation Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive installation documentation for GeneView. Cover Python version requirements (Python 3+), pip installation commands, and dependency management. Document core dependencies (numpy, pandas, matplotlib, scipy, seaborn) and optional dependencies (statsmodels, fastcluster). Include troubleshooting for common installation issues, virtual environment setup recommendations, and verification steps to confirm successful installation. Provide guidance for different operating systems and environments including conda users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Installation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the GeneView installation section focused on step-by-step installation instructions and dependency management. Document the pip installation command for the released version and explain that it automatically installs all dependencies including numpy, pandas, matplotlib, scipy, and seaborn. Include alternative installation methods such as installing from source or development versions. Document system requirements including Python version compatibility (Python 3+). Explain optional dependencies like statsmodels and fastcluster and when they might be beneficial. Include troubleshooting tips for common installation issues such as missing system dependencies or conflicts with existing packages. Provide verification steps to confirm successful installation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Q-Q Plot Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for GeneView's Q-Q plot functionality for P-value distribution analysis in GWAS studies. Explain the theoretical vs observed P-value comparison methodology, lambda inflation factor calculation, and genomic control correction. Document parameter specifications for data input formatting, plot customization options, and statistical significance assessment. Cover the interpretation of Q-Q plots including deviation from the diagonal line, inflation factors, and heterogeneity detection. Include practical examples showing basic Q-Q plots, customized styling, statistical annotations, and integration with GWAS quality control workflows. Address common use cases for population stratification detection, multiple testing corrections, and meta-analysis applications. Provide guidance on statistical interpretations and troubleshooting common issues with P-value distributions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dataset Loading System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for GeneView's dataset loading and management system. Explain the dataset loading workflow including online repository integration for accessing public genomics datasets, local file handling for various genomics formats (VCF, PLINK, .Q files), and format standardization processes. Document caching mechanisms for performance optimization, including temporary file management and cache invalidation strategies. Cover data preprocessing steps including quality control checks, missing data handling, and format conversion utilities. Include practical examples demonstrating dataset loading workflows, error handling for corrupted files, and best practices for managing large genomics datasets. Address memory management considerations and performance optimization techniques for handling big data scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: GWAS Methodology
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Genome-Wide Association Study methodology and statistical concepts. Explain P-value calculations, genome-wide significance thresholds (5×10^-8), and multiple testing corrections. Document Manhattan plot construction, chromosome positioning logic, and SNP annotation strategies. Cover Q-Q plot interpretation for assessing P-value distribution and detecting inflation or deflation. Include guidance on quality control measures, population stratification correction, and replication studies. Provide statistical foundations for understanding GWAS results and limitations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Script Examples
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for GeneView script examples focused on practical implementation and reusable code patterns. Document each script's purpose, input data formats, command-line parameters, and output specifications. Include complete code walkthroughs explaining key functions, parameter choices, and customization options. Provide guidance on adapting scripts for different datasets, batch processing workflows, and integration into larger analysis pipelines. Document best practices for error handling, logging, and performance optimization. Include examples of script modification for specific research questions and data formats.

wiki_catalogs.name: Palette Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for GeneView's color palette management system. Document generate_colors_palette(), circos_pal(), and other palette generation functions with parameter specifications for color scheme selection, customization options, and output formats. Include examples of palette usage in different visualization contexts, integration with matplotlib colormaps, and custom color scheme creation. Document palette naming conventions, color space considerations, and compatibility with scientific visualization standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Population Genetics Visualization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Population Genetics Visualization section focused on ancestral population structure analysis and visualization. Document the admixture coefficient plotting functionality, explaining the biological basis of admixture analysis, .Q file format requirements, and hierarchical clustering integration for sample ordering optimization. Detail the statistical interpretation of admixture coefficients, population structure analysis workflows, and visualization best practices. Include practical examples of data preparation, population group assignment, clustering method selection, and result interpretation. Explain how admixture plots integrate with other population genetics analyses and their role in evolutionary studies, migration pattern analysis, and genetic diversity assessment. Address common challenges in population structure analysis and visualization optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Q-Q Plots
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Q-Q (Quantile-Quantile) plot analysis focused on P-value distribution assessment and statistical significance evaluation. Explain the theoretical versus observed P-value comparison methodology, lambda inflation calculation, and deviation interpretation. Document implementation details including empirical P-value quantile calculation, expected null distribution generation, and confidence interval computation. Cover parameter options for significance thresholds, plot styling, and annotation customization. Include practical examples demonstrating GWAS quality control workflows, population stratification detection, and multiple testing correction assessment. Address common applications in GWAS meta-analysis, cross-study comparison, and replication study validation. Document integration with genomic control methods and heterogeneity analysis approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dataset Loading System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Dataset Loading System focused on GeneView's data ingestion and preprocessing infrastructure. Document the online repository integration capabilities, local data file handling, and format standardization mechanisms. Explain the automatic dataset discovery, caching strategies, and data validation processes. Cover the supported data formats including VCF, PLINK, and population genetics files (.Q files). Include practical examples of dataset loading workflows, data preprocessing pipelines, and error handling strategies. Document the lazy loading mechanisms, memory optimization techniques for large datasets, and data transformation utilities. Address the integration with pandas DataFrame operations, data type inference, and quality control measures. Explain the caching system for frequently accessed datasets and network optimization for remote data retrieval.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create a comprehensive getting started guide for GeneView focusing on quick onboarding for new users. Include step-by-step tutorials for basic Manhattan and Q-Q plot creation using the load_dataset function. Provide minimal working examples that demonstrate core functionality with real genomics data. Cover essential concepts like data loading, basic plotting functions, and customization options. Include first visualization examples with expected outputs and common parameter configurations. Make content accessible to beginners while establishing foundation knowledge for advanced usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quick Start Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the GeneView quick start guide focused on getting users productive quickly with basic visualizations. Document the essential workflow starting with importing geneview, loading sample datasets using load_dataset(), and creating your first Manhattan plot and Q-Q plot. Include complete, runnable code examples for both Manhattan and Q-Q plots with explanations of key parameters. Demonstrate how to customize basic aspects like colors, markers, and labels. Show how to save figures and export high-quality graphics. Include common modifications like rotating x-axis labels, adding titles, and adjusting figure sizes. Provide guidance on understanding the output and interpreting basic visualization results. Keep examples minimal but practical to demonstrate the library's ease of use.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# GeneView: Genomics Data Visualization Library

## Project Introduction

**GeneView** is a Python package designed specifically for creating attractive and informative genomics graphics. Built on top of matplotlib with tight integration to the PyData stack (numpy, pandas), it provides high-level abstractions for structuring grids of plots and specialized functions for visualizing general genomics data.

### Purpose Statement
GeneView aims to simplify the creation of complex genomics visualizations by providing intuitive, high-level plotting functions that handle the technical details while maintaining flexibility for customization.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Provide specialized plotting functions for genomics data analysis
- Offer high-level abstractions for complex multi-panel visualizations
- Maintain seamless integration with the scientific Python ecosystem
- Deliver publication-ready graphics with minimal code complexity

### Target Audience
- Genomic researchers and bioinformaticians
- Data scientists working with genomics data
- Academic institutions and research laboratories
- Anyone requiring sophisticated genomics data visualization tools

## Technical Architecture

The GeneView project follows a modular architecture organized around distinct genomics visualization domains:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[geneview/__init__.py] --> B[gwas/]
    A --> C[popgene/]
    A --> D[baseplot/]
    A --> E[karyotype/]
    A --> F[palette/]
    A --> G[utils/]
    A --> H[algorithm/]
    
    B --> B1[_manhattan.py]
    B --> B2[_qq.py]
    B --> B3[_locuszoom.py]
    
    C --> C1[_admixture.py]
    
    D --> D1[_venn.py]
    
    E --> E1[_karyotype.py]
    
    F --> F1[_palettes.py]
    F --> F2[_circos.py]
    F --> F3[_xkcd_rgb.py]
    
    G --> G1[_dataset.py]
    G --> G2[_decorators.py]
    G --> G3[_adjust_text.py]
    G --> G4[_misc.py]
    
    H --> H1[_cluster.py]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Modules:**
- **gwas/**: Manhattan plots, Q-Q plots, and GWAS-specific visualizations
- **popgene/**: Population genetics visualizations including admixture plots
- **baseplot/**: Fundamental plotting utilities including Venn diagrams
- **karyotype/**: Chromosome ideogram and karyotype visualizations
- **palette/**: Color palette generation and management
- **utils/**: Utility functions, decorators, and dataset handling
- **algorithm/**: Clustering algorithms and statistical computations

### Design Patterns

The project employs several key design patterns:

1. **Facade Pattern**: The main `__init__.py` provides simplified access to all functionality
2. **Factory Pattern**: Dataset loading and color palette generation
3. **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable clustering methods and visualization strategies
4. **Decorator Pattern**: Function wrappers for parameter validation and deprecation handling

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[Dataset Loading]
    B --> C[Data Processing]
    C --> D[Visualization Engine]
    D --> E[Matplotlib Backend]
    E --> F[Output Generation]
    
    G[Color Palettes] --> D
    H[Statistical Methods] --> C
    I[Utility Functions] --> D
    
    J[External Dependencies] --> K[numpy]
    J --> L[pandas]
    J --> M[matplotlib]
    J --> N[scipy]
    J --> O[seaborn]
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Primary Package Interface:**
- `geneview/__init__.py` - Main package initialization and public API exposure
- `setup.py` - Package installation and metadata configuration
- `requirements.txt` - External dependency specifications

**Core Functionality:**
- `geneview/gwas/__init__.py` - GWAS-specific plotting functions
- `geneview/popgene/__init__.py` - Population genetics visualizations
- `geneview/baseplot/__init__.py` - Basic plotting utilities

### Core Modules

**GWAS Visualization (`geneview/gwas/`):**
- `_manhattan.py` - Manhattan plot implementation with customizable thresholds and annotations
- `_qq.py` - Q-Q plot generation for P-value distribution analysis
- `_locuszoom.py` - LocusZoom-style regional association plots

**Population Genetics (`geneview/popgene/`):**
- `_admixture.py` - Admixture coefficient visualization with hierarchical clustering
- Advanced clustering integration with scipy for sample ordering

**Basic Plotting (`geneview/baseplot/`):**
- `_venn.py` - Multi-set Venn diagram generation with custom shapes and labels
- Petal coordinate calculation and shape rendering algorithms

### Configuration Approach

**Package Configuration:**
- Font settings and matplotlib parameter defaults in main `__init__.py`
- Color palette management through dedicated modules
- Dataset loading infrastructure with standardized formats

**External Dependencies:**
- Core: numpy, pandas, matplotlib, scipy, seaborn
- Optional: statsmodels for advanced statistical functions
- Development: pytest for testing infrastructure

### Integration Points

**External Service Integration:**
- Direct integration with matplotlib for rendering
- Pandas DataFrame compatibility for data input
- Seaborn color palette integration for consistent theming

**Data Flow Architecture:**

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant GV as GeneView API
    participant Utils as Utils Module
    participant Plot as Plot Function
    participant MPL as Matplotlib
    
    User->>GV: Call plotting function
    GV->>Utils: Load/process data
    Utils->>Plot: Process data structures
    Plot->>MPL: Render visualization
    MPL-->>User: Display/Save plot
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**GWAS Analysis Tools:**
- Manhattan plots with customizable chromosome positioning and significance thresholds
- Q-Q plots for P-value distribution assessment
- Regional association visualization capabilities

**Population Genetics Visualization:**
- Admixture coefficient plotting with hierarchical clustering
- Population structure analysis and visualization
- Sample ordering optimization for meaningful displays

**General Plotting Utilities:**
- Multi-set Venn diagrams (2-6 sets) with automatic shape calculation
- Flexible color palette generation and management
- Dataset loading and preprocessing utilities

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced Clustering (`geneview/algorithm/_cluster.py`):**
- Hierarchical clustering with multiple linkage methods
- Integration with both scipy and fastcluster backends
- Automatic performance optimization for large datasets

**Flexible Venn Diagrams (`geneview/baseplot/_venn.py`):**
- Mathematical coordinate calculation for optimal shape placement
- Customizable petal labeling and formatting
- Support for complex multi-set intersections

**Robust Data Handling (`geneview/utils/_dataset.py`):**
- Standardized dataset loading interface
- Built-in example datasets for demonstration
- Consistent data format handling across modules

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> DataLoading
    DataLoading --> DataProcessing
    DataProcessing --> Visualization
    Visualization --> OutputGeneration
    OutputGeneration --> [*]
    
    DataProcessing --> StatisticalAnalysis
    StatisticalAnalysis --> DataProcessing
    
    Visualization --> ColorManagement
    ColorManagement --> Visualization
    
    Visualization --> Annotation
    Annotation --> Visualization
```

**Performance Considerations:**
- Vectorized operations using numpy for efficient data processing
- Lazy evaluation where appropriate to minimize computational overhead
- Memory-efficient handling of large genomic datasets
- Caching mechanisms for repeated operations

**Extensibility Features:**
- Modular architecture allowing easy addition of new plot types
- Plugin-style integration with external visualization libraries
- Configurable parameter systems for customization
- Comprehensive error handling and validation

---

- [geneview/baseplot/__init__.py](geneview/baseplot/__init__.py)
- [geneview/karyotype/__init__.py](geneview/karyotype/__init__.py)
- [geneview/palette/__init__.py](geneview/palette/__init__.py)
- [geneview/utils/__init__.py](geneview/utils/__init__.py)

- [geneview/popgene/_admixture.py](geneview/popgene/_admixture.py)

</blog>
